text:
User: deeply review this repo -- I want you to help me with marketing, copywriting, launch strategy, specially positioning -- start with a swarm of sonnet agents to help you gather data, both from the code and from docs (no docs is to be taken too seriously -- might have hallucinations), as well as online market research -- once you've done a first exploration use a fable agent to get high-level ideas, direction, concepts. The first target is to rewrite from scratch the README.md and land high-level copywriting for the landing page, anchored in uniqueness, USP and any best copywriting practices. [...] Assistant: Good grounding. Now I'll launch a parallel research swarm — four Sonnet agents covering code-reality, docs/vision, market landscape, and audience/copy-craft — running concurrently.

Assistant: Swarm is running. While they work, I'll deepen my own read of the product doctrine so I can synthesize sharply when results land. [...] The four research agents (code-reality, docs-vision, market-research, audience-copy) are running in the background. I'll wait for them to land before commissioning the Fable creative-direction pass, since Fable should synthesize across all four plus my doctrine read. I'll pick back up the moment they report. [...] Still waiting on **market-research** (competitive whitespace, A2A/MCP contrast, positioning traps) and **audience-copy** (pain phrasing + a hero-headline swipe file) before I commission the Fable creative-direction pass — Fable should synthesize all four at once. I'll pick up the moment they land.
